value_picks.name: Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF yield = 634.12M / 11.68B = 5.43%, in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 14.55 vs implied PE 400 / 20.37 = 19.64; discount ~25.9%, inside 15-30% borderline.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt position: cash 216M vs debt 2.93B (net -23% of mcap). D/E 56.95 (50-150 band) and current ratio 1.191 (>0.8). Borderline.

dividend.detail: Yield 1.86% (below 2% pass cutoff) with low payout 35.48% — sustainable but not premium.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 93.3% (>75%) and recommendation_mean 2.25 (<2.5). 5 buys / 7 holds / 0 sells across 12 analysts.

ai_exposure.detail: Core business is naval shipbuilding (aircraft carriers, submarines, amphibious assault). Mission Technologies segment touches autonomous/unmanned systems and C4ISR where AI is incremental, but AI is neither a primary growth driver nor an existential threat to a regulated naval prime.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest is light (2.63% of float, 1.52 days to cover) and falling (-12.86% MoM), suggesting bears are covering into the drawdown. Beta 0.287 means the move is idiosyncratic, not market-driven.

catalysts_general: Trading ~26% below analyst median target of $400 (low end of analyst range $298 is at current price, signaling consensus floor).
Revenue growth 13.4% TTM with shipbuilding backlog visibility — defense prime cycle support.
Price 18.4% below SMA20 and 27.4% below SMA50 — extended oversold from trend.
Net debt of ~$2.7B is sizable but serviceable on $1.13B EBITDA (~2.4x net leverage).
Dividend payout only 35.5% leaves room for buybacks or yield growth.
Beta 0.287 — defensive, decorrelated from broad market.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of recent support; sector derating on margin compression headlines.
Capitulation toward midpoint between current and 52w low on contract execution miss.
Revisit 52w low on defense budget uncertainty or major program delay (e.g., submarine cost overrun).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below analyst low target ($298) and ~7% below current; gives a ~9% buffer above 52w low while keeping FCF yield closer to 6%. Aligns with low end of analyst range as a structural floor.

comparables.note: General Dynamics — closest naval/submarine comp (Electric Boat); trades at premium PE despite similar growth (not in input data).
Lockheed Martin — broader defense prime; HII trades at notable discount (not in input data).
Northrop Grumman — adjacent defense prime with submarine exposure via subcontracting (not in input data).

value_picks.analyst_take: HII offers a defense-prime franchise at 14.5x forward earnings with a 26% discount to analyst consensus, 35% drawdown, and very low beta (0.287). The setup is supportive but not exceptional — net debt of $2.7B and 35.5% payout limit the cushion, and the score of 4.0/6 reflects a name that is broadly attractive on each criterion but rarely outright cheap. WATCHLIST with a target entry near $275 where FCF yield approaches 6% and the analyst low-target floor compresses.

value_picks.name: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 11.68 vs implied PE 80 / 5.37 = 14.90; discount ~21.6%, inside 15-30% borderline.

drawdown.detail: Down 30.17% from 52w high ($89.77 -> $62.69) — between 20-35% borderline band.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt: cash 2.26B vs debt 10.58B (net -29% of mcap). D/E 97.06 (50-150 band) and current ratio 1.222 (>0.8). Borderline.

dividend.detail: Yield 0.22% (token) with payout 4.2% — sustainable but immaterial; meets borderline criteria of yield>0 and payout<90%.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 91.7% (>75%) and recommendation_mean 1.83 (<2.5). 12 buys / 6 holds / 0 sells across 18 analysts.

ai_exposure.detail: Imaging and Advanced Visualization Solutions segments are direct beneficiaries of AI-assisted diagnostics (CT, MR, ultrasound), and Pharmaceutical Diagnostics intersects with AI-driven precision medicine workflows. Medical imaging AI is one of the most commercially mature healthcare AI verticals.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 3.03% with 2.04 days to cover and essentially flat MoM (+0.32%) — no directional positioning signal. Beta 0.868 implies more market sensitivity than HII; weakness has been partly macro-driven.

catalysts_general: Trading ~22% below analyst median target of $80 (low target $65 is just above current price — strong analyst floor).
Forward PE 11.68 vs trailing 15.03 implies analysts model meaningful earnings recovery (forward EPS $5.37 vs trailing $4.17, +29%).
ROE 19.46% and gross margin 39.1% — quality medical-device profile despite the drawdown.
Current price 62.69 essentially at SMA20 (62.49) but 6.8% below SMA50 — base-building behavior near 52w low.
Revenue 21.0B with 7.4% growth; AI/imaging upgrade cycle remains intact.
12 of 18 analysts rate Buy/Strong Buy; 0 Sell ratings — clean consensus.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Re-test 52w low on tariff/China imaging exposure or hospital capex pause.
Break of 52w low on guidance cut tied to FX or Pharmaceutical Diagnostics softness.
Deeper derating on margin compression or competitive loss in CT/MR refresh cycle.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52w low ($58.75) and below analyst low target ($65) — compresses forward PE under 11x and lifts FCF yield toward 5.5%, providing a defined structural support level for entry.

comparables.note: Medtronic — broader medtech with similar diversification; GEHC trades at material discount (not in input data).
Stryker — premium medtech multiple highlights GEHC's relative cheapness (not in input data).
Philips — closest imaging comparable; GEHC's PE discount reflects similar end markets but cleaner balance-sheet narrative (not in input data).

value_picks.analyst_take: GEHC trades at 11.7x forward earnings with the cleanest analyst tape (12/18 Buy, recommendation_mean 1.83) of either name, and the AI-imaging tailwind is the strongest single structural argument across both tickers. The 3.5/6 score reflects a higher-leverage balance sheet (D/E 97) and shallower drawdown that hasn't yet delivered a deep-value entry. WATCHLIST with a target entry near $59.50 — at or just inside the 52w-low floor — where the implied analyst discount widens to ~25% and the risk/reward against the $80 median target inflects.

growth_picks.name: Gen Digital Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 27.0% sits in the 15-30% borderline band - solid but not hypergrowth for a software peer.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 78.46% comfortably exceeds the 65% software-quality bar - consistent with a recurring-subscription consumer cyber model.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 27.0 (rev growth) + 63.4 (op margin) = 90.4, well above the 50 threshold and exceptional for a mature platform.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 265.2% vastly outpaces revenue growth 27.0%, evidencing strong operating leverage post-Avast integration.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 39.9% is excellent, though it is amplified by high leverage (D/E 316%) on a small book value of $4.37.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 5.89% sits under the 10% cap but is rising (+8.41% MoM), meeting the borderline criterion of '<10% OR declining'.

ai_exposure.detail: Gen Digital's Norton/LifeLock/Avast portfolio benefits as AI-driven phishing, deepfakes, and identity fraud expand the consumer attack surface; they have also marketed Genie AI scam-detection across their brands. Marginal downside is commoditization of basic antivirus by free AI tools, but identity/financial protection (the higher-ARPU bundle) is structurally aided by AI threats.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals provided in input. Analyst tape is neutral (recommendation_mean 2.09, 5 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell of 11) with a median target of $27.00 essentially matching spot - suggesting consensus views current levels as fair value, not a deal-radar setup.

catalysts_general: Forward P/E 8.45 is roughly half of trailing P/E 17.68 - the market is pricing in a step-change in EPS to ~$3.29 forward vs $1.57 trailing
Earnings growth 265.2% vs revenue growth 27% implies a margin/synergy inflection (consistent with Avast deal integration)
Free cash flow $1.42B against $16.7B market cap = ~8.5% FCF yield, attractive for a software business
Stock up 41.4% in 20 days and 32.4% in 50 days - rapid mean-reversion / momentum thrust off the $17.78 low
Analyst mean target $30.01 = only 8.1% upside; high target $46 vs low $22 implies wide outcome dispersion
1.8% dividend yield with 31.85% payout ratio leaves room for sustained capital return

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mean-reversion to 20-day SMA after the 41% 20-day surge cools
Pullback to 50-day SMA on broad software de-rating or guidance reset
Retest of 52-week low on debt-anxiety flare (D/E 316%, current ratio 0.40) or consumer-cyber churn surprise

ideal_entry.reasoning: Buying after a 41% 20-day rip is poor risk/reward; wait for a pullback toward the 20-day SMA ($23.35) which still implies <7.2x forward P/E and offers a margin of safety against the leveraged balance sheet.

comparables.note: Predecessor entity to GEN - same consumer-cyber DNA prior to Avast merger
Not in input - enterprise cyber, vastly higher multiple but similar revenue growth pace; highlights how cheap GEN trades for comparable top-line
Not in input - enterprise network security; trades at 4x GEN's multiple on slower growth, underscoring GEN's value-vs-growth profile
Not in input - enterprise SASE/SecOps; benchmark for what scaled cyber commands when growth is platform-led

growth_picks.analyst_take: GEN scores 5.0/6 on the Growth framework with an exceptional Rule of 40 (90.4), 78% gross margins, and a forward P/E of just 8.45 - a rare combination indicating the market still treats the post-Avast entity as ex-growth despite 27% revenue and 265% earnings growth. The catch is balance-sheet fragility (D/E 316%, current ratio 0.40) and a 41% 20-day rip that has consumed most of the consensus upside (target $30 vs spot $27.76). I'd rate WATCHLIST: the franchise quality merits ownership, but waiting for a pullback to the $23-24 area offers materially better risk/reward.
